---
title: "MasterClothe Project"
author: "Kailani Niziol"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
```

```{r}
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(lubridate)
path <- file.choose()
#excel sheets 
customers   <- read_excel("MasterFiles_v2.xlsx", sheet = "Customer Master")
stores      <- read_excel("MasterFiles_v2.xlsx", sheet = "Store Master")
competitors <- read_excel("MasterFiles_v2.xlsx", sheet = "Competitor Master")
df          <- read.csv("Scenario2_Data.csv")

#dates
df$date                 <- as.Date(df$date)
competitors$opening_day <- as.Date(competitors$opening_day)

df <- df %>%
  left_join(customers, by = "customer_id")

#customer purchasing history
customer_history <- df %>%
  group_by(customer_id) %>%
  summarise(
    num_stores = n_distinct(store_id),
    shop_visit = n(),
    .groups    = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(shopping_type = ifelse(num_stores == 1,
                                "Master Clothe Exclusive",
                                "Other Stores")) %>%
  left_join(select(customers, customer_id, loyalty),
            by = "customer_id")       

#loyalty summary
loyalty_summary <- customer_history %>%
  group_by(shopping_type) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_loyalty = mean(loyalty, na.rm = TRUE),
    count       = n()
  )

#plot 1 customer loyalty 
ggplot(loyalty_summary,
       aes(x = shopping_type, y = avg_loyalty, fill = shopping_type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(avg_loyalty, 1)),
            vjust = -0.5, size = 5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Master Clothe Exclusive"  = "lightsteelblue3",
                               "Other Stores"  = "thistle3")) +
  ylim(0, max(loyalty_summary$avg_loyalty)*1.2) +
  labs(title = "Customer Loyalty: MasterClothe Excluse vs Non-Exclusive Shoppers",
       x     = NULL,
       y     = "Average Loyalty Score") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(lubridate)


#excel data
customers  <- read_excel("MasterFiles_v2.xlsx", sheet = "Customer Master")
stores      <- read_excel("MasterFiles_v2.xlsx", sheet = "Store Master")
competitors <- read_excel("MasterFiles_v2.xlsx", sheet = "Competitor Master")
df          <- read.csv("Scenario2_Data.csv")

#date formatting
stores$opening_date     <- as.Date(stores$opening_date)
competitors$opening_day <- as.Date(competitors$opening_day)
df$date                 <- as.Date(df$date)

#customer info
df <- df %>%
  left_join(customers, by = "customer_id")

#competitor opening date
first_comp_date <- min(competitors$opening_day)

#customer history 
customer_history <- df %>%
  group_by(customer_id) %>%
  summarise(
    first_purchase  = min(date),
    last_purchase   = max(date),
    total_visits    = n(),
    shops_at_master = any(store_id %in% stores$store_id),
    num_stores      = n_distinct(store_id),
    .groups         = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    customer_type = ifelse(first_purchase < first_comp_date,
                           "Long Term", "New Customer"),
    
    shopping_type = ifelse(num_stores == 1,
                           "Master Clothe Only",
                           "Other Stores")
  ) %>%
  left_join(select(customers, customer_id, loyalty, income),
            by = "customer_id")

#customer income grouping 
customer_history <- customer_history %>%
  mutate(income_group = cut(income,
                            breaks = c(0, 40000, 80000,
                                       120000, 200000, Inf),
                            labels = c("Under $40k", "$40k-$80k",
                                       "$80k-$120k", "$120k-$200k",
                                       "$200k+")))


#plot2 for long and new customer 
longterm_shopping <- customer_history %>%
  group_by(customer_type, shopping_type) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(customer_type) %>%
  mutate(pct = round(count / sum(count) * 100, 1))

ggplot(longterm_shopping,
       aes(x = customer_type, y = pct, fill = shopping_type)) +
  geom_bar(stat     = "identity",
           position = "dodge",
           width    = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(pct, "%")),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.6),
            vjust    = -0.5,
            size     = 4,
            fontface = "bold") +
 
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Master Clothe Only" = "lightsteelblue3",
                               "Other Stores"  = "thistle3")) +
  ylim(0, 110) +
  labs(title = "Long Term vs New Customers: Shopping Preference",
       x     = NULL,
       y     = "Percentage of Customers (%)",
       fill  = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

#plot3 for average of loyalty by income group
income_loyalty <- customer_history %>%
  group_by(income_group) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_loyalty = mean(loyalty, na.rm = TRUE),
    count       = n()
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(income_group))

ggplot(income_loyalty,
       aes(x = income_group, y = avg_loyalty, fill = income_group)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.6, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(avg_loyalty, 1)),
            vjust = -0.5, size = 4.5, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Pastel1") +
  ylim(0, max(income_loyalty$avg_loyalty) * 1.2) +
  labs(title    = "Average Loyalty Score by Income Group",
       x        = "Income Group",
       y        = "Average Loyalty Score") +
  theme_minimal()

#plot4 shopping preference by income group
income_preference <- customer_history %>%
  group_by(income_group, shopping_type) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(income_group) %>%
  mutate(pct = round(count / sum(count) * 100, 1)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(income_group))

ggplot(income_preference,
       aes(x = income_group, y = pct, fill = shopping_type)) +
  geom_bar(stat     = "identity",
           position = "dodge",
           width    = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(pct, "%")),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.6),
            vjust    = -0.5,
            size     = 3.5,
            fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Master Clothe Only" = "lightsteelblue3",
                               "Other Stores"        = "thistle3")) +
  ylim(0, 100) +
  labs(title = "Shopping Preference Based off Income",
       x     = "Income Group",
       y     = "Percentage of Customers",
       fill  = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```
```{r}
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

#data loaded
customers <- read_excel("MasterFiles_v2.xlsx", sheet = "Customer Master")

# sort by age group 
customers <- customers %>%
  mutate(age_group = cut(age,
                         breaks = c(0, 25, 35, 45, 55, 65, 75, 100),
                         labels = c("18-25", "26-35", "36-45",
                                    "46-55", "56-65", "66-75",
                                    "75 and older")))

# plot for age group and number of customers 
 ggplot(age_loyalty,
             aes(x = age_group, y = count, fill = age_group)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.6, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = count),
            vjust = -0.5, size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Pastel1") +
  ylim(0, max(age_loyalty$count) * 1.2) +
  labs(title    = "Number of Customers by Age Group",
       x        = "Age Group",
       y        = "Number of Customers") +
  theme_minimal() 




```


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
```
```{r}
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(lubridate)

# --- Load data ---
customers   <- read_excel("MasterFiles_v2.xlsx", sheet = "Customer Master")
stores      <- read_excel("MasterFiles_v2.xlsx", sheet = "Store Master")
competitors <- read_excel("MasterFiles_v2.xlsx", sheet = "Competitor Master")
df          <- read.csv("Scenario2_Data.csv")

# --- Fix dates ---
df$date                 <- as.Date(df$date)
competitors$opening_day <- as.Date(competitors$opening_day)

# --- Join customers onto transactions ---
df <- df %>%
  left_join(customers, by = "customer_id")

# --- Build customer history ---
customer_history <- df %>%
  group_by(customer_id) %>%
  summarise(
    num_stores = n_distinct(store_id),
    shop_visit = n(),
    .groups    = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(shopping_type = ifelse(num_stores == 1,
                                "Master Clothe Exclusive",
                                "Other Stores")) %>%
  left_join(select(customers, customer_id, loyalty),
            by = "customer_id")

# --- Loyalty summary ---
loyalty_summary <- customer_history %>%
  group_by(shopping_type) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_loyalty = mean(loyalty, na.rm = TRUE),
    count       = n()
  )

# --- Plot 1: Customer loyalty exclusive vs other ---
ggplot(loyalty_summary,
       aes(x = shopping_type, y = avg_loyalty, fill = shopping_type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(avg_loyalty, 1)),
            vjust = -0.5, size = 5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Master Clothe Exclusive" = "lightsteelblue3",
                               "Other Stores"             = "thistle3")) +
  ylim(0, max(loyalty_summary$avg_loyalty) * 1.2) +
  labs(title = "Customer Loyalty: Master Clothe Exclusive vs Non-Exclusive Shoppers",
       x     = NULL,
       y     = "Average Loyalty Score") +
  theme_minimal()
```
```{r}
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(lubridate)

# --- Load data ---
customers   <- read_excel("MasterFiles_v2.xlsx", sheet = "Customer Master")
stores      <- read_excel("MasterFiles_v2.xlsx", sheet = "Store Master")
competitors <- read_excel("MasterFiles_v2.xlsx", sheet = "Competitor Master")
df          <- read.csv("Scenario2_Data.csv")

# --- Fix dates ---
stores$opening_date     <- as.Date(stores$opening_date)
competitors$opening_day <- as.Date(competitors$opening_day)
df$date                 <- as.Date(df$date)

# --- Join customers onto transactions ---
df <- df %>%
  left_join(customers, by = "customer_id")

# --- First competitor opening date ---
first_comp_date <- min(competitors$opening_day)

# --- Build customer history ---
customer_history <- df %>%
  group_by(customer_id) %>%
  summarise(
    first_purchase  = min(date),
    last_purchase   = max(date),
    total_visits    = n(),
    shops_at_master = any(store_id %in% stores$store_id),
    num_stores      = n_distinct(store_id),
    .groups         = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    customer_type = ifelse(first_purchase < first_comp_date,
                           "Long Term", "New Customer"),
    shopping_type = ifelse(num_stores == 1,
                           "Master Clothe Only",
                           "Other Stores")
  ) %>%
  left_join(select(customers, customer_id, loyalty, income),
            by = "customer_id")

# --- Add income groups ---
customer_history <- customer_history %>%
  mutate(income_group = cut(income,
                            breaks = c(0, 40000, 80000,
                                       120000, 200000, Inf),
                            labels = c("Under $40k", "$40k-$80k",
                                       "$80k-$120k", "$120k-$200k",
                                       "$200k+")))

# --- Plot 2: Long term vs new customer shopping preference ---
longterm_shopping <- customer_history %>%
  group_by(customer_type, shopping_type) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(customer_type) %>%
  mutate(pct = round(count / sum(count) * 100, 1))

ggplot(longterm_shopping,
       aes(x = customer_type, y = pct, fill = shopping_type)) +
  geom_bar(stat     = "identity",
           position = "dodge",
           width    = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(pct, "%")),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.6),
            vjust    = -0.5,
            size     = 4,
            fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Master Clothe Only" = "lightsteelblue3",
                               "Other Stores"        = "thistle3")) +
  ylim(0, 110) +
  labs(title = "Long Term vs New Customers: Shopping Preference",
       x     = NULL,
       y     = "Percentage of Customers (%)",
       fill  = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

# --- Plot 3: Average loyalty by income group ---
income_loyalty <- customer_history %>%
  group_by(income_group) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_loyalty = mean(loyalty, na.rm = TRUE),
    count       = n()
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(income_group))

ggplot(income_loyalty,
       aes(x = income_group, y = avg_loyalty, fill = income_group)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.6, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(avg_loyalty, 1)),
            vjust = -0.5, size = 4.5, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Pastel1") +
  ylim(0, max(income_loyalty$avg_loyalty) * 1.2) +
  labs(title = "Average Loyalty Score by Income Group",
       x     = "Income Group",
       y     = "Average Loyalty Score") +
  theme_minimal()

# --- Plot 4: Shopping preference by income group ---
income_preference <- customer_history %>%
  group_by(income_group, shopping_type) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(income_group) %>%
  mutate(pct = round(count / sum(count) * 100, 1)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(income_group))

ggplot(income_preference,
       aes(x = income_group, y = pct, fill = shopping_type)) +
  geom_bar(stat     = "identity",
           position = "dodge",
           width    = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(pct, "%")),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.6),
            vjust    = -0.5,
            size     = 3.5,
            fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Master Clothe Only" = "lightsteelblue3",
                               "Other Stores"        = "thistle3")) +
  ylim(0, 110) +
  labs(title = "Shopping Preference Based off Income",
       x     = "Income Group",
       y     = "Percentage of Customers",
       fill  = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```
```{r}
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# --- Load data ---
# FIX: customers reloaded fresh at the top of this chunk
# so it is available cleanly without any joins from previous chunks
customers <- read_excel("MasterFiles_v2.xlsx", sheet = "Customer Master")

# --- Add age groups ---
customers <- customers %>%
  mutate(age_group = cut(age,
                         breaks = c(0, 25, 35, 45, 55, 65, 75, 100),
                         labels = c("18-25", "26-35", "36-45",
                                    "46-55", "56-65", "66-75",
                                    "75 and older")))

# --- Plot 5: Avg loyalty by age group ---
age_loyalty <- customers %>%
  group_by(age_group) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_loyalty = mean(loyalty, na.rm = TRUE),
    count       = n()
  )

ggplot(age_loyalty,
       aes(x = age_group, y = avg_loyalty, fill = age_group)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.6, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(avg_loyalty, 1)),
            vjust = -0.5, size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0("n=", count)),
            vjust = 1.5, size = 3,
            color = "white", fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Blues") +
  ylim(0, max(age_loyalty$avg_loyalty) * 1.2) +
  labs(title    = "Average Loyalty Score by Age Group",
       subtitle = "Which age group is most loyal to Master Clothe?",
       x        = "Age Group",
       y        = "Average Loyalty Score") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "gray50", size = 10))

# --- Plot 6: Customer count by age group ---
ggplot(age_loyalty,
       aes(x = age_group, y = count, fill = age_group)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.6, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = count),
            vjust = -0.5, size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Blues") +
  ylim(0, max(age_loyalty$count) * 1.2) +
  labs(title    = "Number of Customers by Age Group",
       subtitle = "Which age group has the most customers?",
       x        = "Age Group",
       y        = "Number of Customers") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "gray50", size = 10))

# --- Plot 7: Avg income by age group ---
age_income <- customers %>%
  group_by(age_group) %>%
  summarise(avg_income = mean(income, na.rm = TRUE))

ggplot(age_income,
       aes(x = age_group, y = avg_income, fill = age_group)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.6, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0("$", round(avg_income / 1000, 0), "k")),
            vjust = -0.5, size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Blues") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::dollar_format()) +
  ylim(0, max(age_income$avg_income) * 1.2) +
  labs(title    = "Average Income by Age Group",
       subtitle = "Which age group earns the most?",
       x        = "Age Group",
       y        = "Average Annual Income ($)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "gray50", size = 10))
```
